name: save-the-cat
description: Blake Snyder's legendary screenwriting guide — the 15-beat story structure, the 'Save the Cat' principle, the logline formula, and the immutable laws of screenplay physics that have helped countless writers break into Hollywood.
useCase: Learning professional screenwriting structure, crafting marketable loglines, building a beat sheet, developing characters audiences love, and navigating the Hollywood spec market.
knownRisks: Written for the commercial Hollywood market — not for art-house or experimental filmmaking
Some examples are dated (published 2005, references to early 2000s films)
Highly formulaic — some critics argue the 'beat sheet' approach stifles creativity
Snyder's tone is brash and self-assured, which may put off some readers
